dimensionDescriptions.detection_logic: Explain what the prioritized rule or alert is intended to detect and which thresholds, fields, or timing assumptions influence its meaning.
dimensionDescriptions.telemetry_quality: Explain which telemetry fields or sources may be incomplete, delayed, or inconsistent and how this affects interpretation.
dimensionDescriptions.false_positive_management: Discuss possible noise drivers and how suppression, enrichment, or contextual logic might reduce unnecessary alerts.
dimensionDescriptions.false_negative_risk: Explain what attacker behaviors could be missed after tuning or suppression decisions.
dimensionDescriptions.tuning_action: Explain the specific tuning adjustment being proposed and why it is a reasonable improvement given the evidence.
dimensionDescriptions.detection_gap: Describe a visibility gap that may have limited detection capability.
dimensionDescriptions.investigation_pivot: Describe one or two investigative checks that could clarify whether current alerts represent real threat activity.
dimensionDescriptions.mitigation_tradeoff: Explain operational impacts or tradeoffs created by the proposed response or tuning action.
dimensionDescriptions.risk_reasoning: Explain why the chosen risk level follows logically from the observed signals and potential impact.
dimensionDescriptions.confidence_reasoning: Explain why the chosen confidence level reflects the quality of available evidence and remaining uncertainty.
dimensionDescriptions.evidence_linkage: Explain which specific evidence items support key conclusions.
dimensionDescriptions.hypothesis_alignment: Explain how the final assessment aligns with the decision path taken during the simulation.

reportSections.title: Executive Summary
reportSections.template: Briefly summarize the situation: what the SIEM alerts indicate, the most likely interpretation of the activity, and the immediate recommendation.

reportSections.title: Detection Logic Interpretation
reportSections.template: Explain what the relevant correlation rules or alerts are designed to detect and which thresholds or contextual signals influence their reliability.

reportSections.title: Signal vs Noise Assessment
reportSections.template: Explain why certain alerts appear meaningful signals while others may represent benign or low-confidence activity.

reportSections.title: Telemetry Quality Assessment
reportSections.template: Identify telemetry limitations such as missing fields, ingestion delays, or inconsistent normalization that affect detection accuracy.

reportSections.title: Tuning Recommendation
reportSections.template: Describe a specific detection improvement or rule adjustment and explain why it is preferable to alternative tuning options.

reportSections.title: Operational Tradeoffs
reportSections.template: Explain the potential operational consequences of the proposed tuning or response actions.

reportSections.title: Next Investigative Steps
reportSections.template: Identify one or two investigative checks that would most effectively validate or challenge the current assessment.

reportSections.title: Professional Conclusion
reportSections.template: Close with concise next steps and explain how analysts should reassess the situation if new evidence appears.

gapRules.prompt: You may want to explain the most significant visibility or telemetry gap and how it affected the reliability of the alerts.
Consider proposing one practical tuning adjustment and explaining how it could improve detection quality.
You may want to discuss at least one operational tradeoff associated with the recommended response or tuning change.
It may help to explain why the selected confidence level fits the available evidence and remaining uncertainty.
